new_classes.definition_en: Beverage produced by the fermentation of fresh grapes, including wines fortified with alcohol (e.g. port, sherry), classified under CN heading 2204. Excludes grape must of heading 2009.
Wine of fresh grapes to which distilled alcohol has been added to increase the alcoholic strength, classified under CN heading 2204.
Unfermented or partially fermented liquid obtained from fresh grapes, classified under CN heading 2204 (as opposed to grape must of heading 2009 which is classified elsewhere).
The biological process by which sugars in fresh grape juice are converted to ethyl alcohol and carbon dioxide by yeast, producing wine of fresh grapes classified under CN heading 2204.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria are covered: (1) wine of fresh grapes as a material entity produced by grape fermentation, (2) fortified wine as a subclass enriched with alcohol, (3) grape must of heading 2204 as distinct from grape must of heading 2009 (FruitOrVegetableJuice2009). The disjointness between 2204 grape must and heading 2009 juice is expressed via complement restrictions. The reference to 'actual alcoholic strength by volume' (Additional Note 2(a) to Chapter 22) is already covered by the existing TBox property eucn:alcoholByVolumePercent, so no new property is needed. A minor gap remains: the legal note references the Additional Note 2(a) definitional criterion for 'actual alcoholic strength by volume' but the note text itself is not available here to formalise its exact threshold semantics — hence coverage is 0.85 rather than 1.0.
